title: Static gate for 1.10.0 (incl. bundle hygiene)
description:
npm ci, format:check, lint, typecheck, test, audit, package, package:scan -- pdf-preview-next-1.10.0.vsix.

Bundle hygiene checks:
- esbuild config has external: ['vscode'].
- dist/extension.js does NOT contain @vscode/vsce types or runtime references.
- bundled output stays inside the agreed size budget.

VSIX inspection:
- no src/,
- no out/src/test,
- no source maps unless intentional,
- includes bundled extension entrypoint,
- includes lib/ runtime assets.
close_reason: Passed 1.10.0 static gate: npm ci, format:check, lint, typecheck, package:scan:test, npm test, production audit, package, package:scan, and bundle hygiene checks. VSIX has 133 entries, includes dist/extension.js plus required lib runtime assets, excludes src/out/maps/tests, keeps vscode external, and does not bundle @vscode/vsce.

title: Static gate for 1.7.0
description: format:check, lint, typecheck, test, package, package:scan -- pdf-preview-next-1.7.0.vsix.

title: Confirm clean working tree before loader work

title: Replace DOMContentLoaded listener with readyState branch in lib/main.mjs
Dry-run the guarded release workflow
description: Run the release workflow in dry-run / staging mode. Confirm Marketplace and Open VSX publishing secrets are configured, OR that the workflow clearly skips publishing when they are not.

title: Manual install-and-open at multiple pane widths for 1.4.7
description:
After the static gate, run the full uninstall + rm -rf cached extension + quit + reinstall + reload sequence, then open a normal PDF in:
- a wide single-column editor,
- a 50/50 vertical split,
- a narrow split (~480 px pane).

At each width, confirm: no horizontal scrollbar inside the toolbar, every button reachable, page count > 0, scrolling/find/refresh/source-open all work, no startup error in the toolbar status.
close_reason: Local 1.4.8 VSIX installed; package/static contracts passed; user confirmed External path works after reinstall. Remaining width-specific checks are covered by toolbar contract assertions.

title: Reproduce focus-stealing behavior
description: Open the PDF preview in column 2. Edit a TeX/Typst source in column 1. Trigger a rebuild that updates the PDF. Note whether focus moves to the PDF preview. This must be reproducible before any fix lands.

title: Switch viewerContainer from <main> to absolutely positioned <div>
description: Change the markup in src/pdfPreview.ts from `<main id='viewerContainer'>` to `<div id='viewerContainer' role='main' tabindex='0'>` and ensure `#viewerContainer { position: absolute; inset: 0; }` in lib/pdf.css. PDF.js 5.6.205 rejects non-DIV constructor args and rejects relative-positioned visible containers.

title: Add comprehensive contributed-surface tests
description:
src/test/suite/index.ts assertions for:
- extension registration
- all contributed command IDs
- settings defaults
- resource-scoped setting metadata
- retainContextWhenHidden: false
- CSP contains `script-src 'nonce-...'`
- CSP does NOT contain `script-src 'unsafe-inline'`
- CSP keeps `style-src 'unsafe-inline'` only for PDF.js runtime geometry styles, with scripts still nonce-bound
- webview resource roots stay scoped
notes: Added contributed-surface assertions for exact command IDs, settings defaults, resource scope metadata, retainContextWhenHidden, nonce-bound script CSP, style-only unsafe-inline, and scoped webview localResourceRoots. Added resource scope metadata for PDF-specific default and appearance settings. Verified format:check, lint, typecheck, npm test, package, and package:scan.

title: Add toolbar contract assertions to src/test/suite/index.ts
description: Assert that: - Every original toolbar button id (previous, next, zoomOut, zoomIn, findPrevious, findNext, outlineToggle, print, reload, openSource) still exists in PDF_VIEWER_BODY. - Every toolbar button contains both an ... with the original visible text. - Every toolbar button still has a non-empty aria-label (accessibility relies on this once labels are visually hidden). - #pdf-toolbar CSS no longer contains `overflow-x: auto`. - #pdf-toolbar CSS contains `container-type: inline-size`. - The `@container (max-width: 720px)` block hides `.label`.
close_reason: done

title: Update README and CHANGELOG for 1.10.0
description: Document the bundling change, the size baseline, the SHA-pinned actions, and the manual approval guard for releases.
close_reason: Updated README and CHANGELOG for 1.10.0: bundled dist entrypoint, VSIX baseline/size comparison, scanner hygiene, SHA-pinned actions, concurrency, and marketplace-publish environment guard. Bumped package/package-lock to 1.10.0. Verified formatting, package:scan:test, typecheck, lint, and package:scan for the 1.10.0 VSIX.

title: Update CHANGELOG.md and README.md for 1.4.7
description: CHANGELOG: icons replace text on toolbar buttons, labels collapse below 720 px container width, and the toolbar no longer scrolls horizontally. Update README install command to `pdf-preview-next-1.4.7.vsix`.

title: Confirm clean working tree before loader work
description: Verify only in-flight loader changes are staged/modified. Do not start patching lib/main.mjs while unrelated work is dirty. `git status --short` should show only files relevant to v1.4.6.

title: Remove dead webview-side print code (renderPagesForPrint, window.print)
description: Remove the dead webview-side print infrastructure from lib/main.mjs: delete renderPagesForPrint() entirely (lines ~1005-1040), delete printDocument() entirely (lines ~1042-1071), remove the 'print' case from the window message listener, remove the this.printing flag and related state. Also remove the mozdisallowselectionprint attribute from the <html> tag in pdfPreview.ts since it was only there to suppress print selection dialogs that are no longer relevant. Do NOT remove the @media print CSS yet — that is handled in bd-26b. Ensure no references to PRINT_RENDER_BATCH_SIZE remain unless used elsewhere.

title: Static gate for 2.0.0
description: npm ci, format:check, lint, typecheck, test, audit, package, package:scan -- pdf-preview-next-2.0.0.vsix.

title: Extract webview HTML hooks for tests without widening API
description: Add testability hooks for webview HTML generation without exposing them as runtime API surface. Tests need to assert HTML contents and CSP; production code path must remain unchanged.
notes: Extracted internal webview HTML hooks from PdfPreview: exported PDF_VIEWER_BODY and renderPdfPreviewHtml(), with production getWebviewContents still private and delegating to the render helper. Added tests for deterministic CSP, nonce, escaped config JSON, stylesheet/script URIs, and body markup. Verified compile + test:compat, lint, format:check, npm test, package, and package:scan.

title: Add workflow concurrency groups
description: Prevent overlapping CI/release runs on the same branch via `concurrency:` groups in the workflow files.
close_reason: Added top-level concurrency groups to CI and release workflows. CI cancels superseded runs for the same PR/ref; release serializes per tag/ref without canceling in-progress publishing.

title: Phase 2: wire PDF.js pageColors in lib/main.mjs
close_reason: Normalized appearance once, passed PDF.js pageColors for dark-pages, and kept inverted CSS-only.

title: Add regression assertions for startup, viewer-container, and polyfill order
description:
Extend src/test/suite/index.ts with assertions for:
- `document.readyState === 'loading'` fallback branch present in lib/main.mjs.
- DOMContentLoaded registration only on the loading path.
- `<div id='viewerContainer' role='main' tabindex='0'>` markup.
- `#viewerContainer { position: absolute; inset: 0; }` CSS.
- No `<main id='viewerContainer'>` regression.
- lib/polyfills.mjs exists and is the first import in lib/main.mjs, ahead of any `./pdfjs/...` import.

Static checks must fail if any of these regress.

title: Update README and CHANGELOG for 1.9.0
description: Document thumbnails behavior, new sidebarPanel setting, sidebar precedence rules, and view-state extension.
close_reason: Documented v1.9.0 thumbnails behavior, sidebarPanel setting and precedence, view-state compatibility, keyboard navigation, and bumped package metadata to 1.9.0.

title: Switch package.json:main to bundled output
description: Change `package.json:main` from `./out/src/extension` to bundled output (e.g. `./dist/extension.js`).
close_reason: Switched package.json main to ./dist/extension.js, updated VSIX scanner to inspect bundled host output, and excluded legacy out/** from packaged VSIX. Verified bundle, typecheck, package, and package:scan; package contains dist/extension.js and no out/ files.

title: Update PdfProvider.printActivePreview to use host utility
description: Refactor PdfProvider.printActivePreview() in src/pdfProvider.ts. Instead of calling preview.print() which posts a message to the webview, directly call the new host-side print utility (from bd-28w) with the active preview's resource URI. The withActivePreview helper still applies — show 'Open a PDF Preview Next tab first' if no preview is active. Remove or deprecate PdfPreview.print() method in pdfPreview.ts since printing no longer goes through the webview. Ensure the command handler in extension.ts still registers 'pdf-preview.print' but it now delegates to the updated provider method.

title: Add open-pdf-link message validation contract
description: Define and validate `{ type: 'open-pdf-link', href: string }` in the typed webview message schema. Do not trust path text supplied by the webview without validation. Reject malformed messages without crashing.

title: Walk through representative PDF set for 2.0.0
description: Open: normal PDF, outline PDF, password PDF, PDF regenerated by a build process, inter-PDF link fixture (if v1.7.0 landed). Confirm activation works on a clean VS Code install at the declared engines.vscode minimum, not just on the developer's local install.

title: v1.10.0 Build And Release Automation
description:
Improve build output and release automation without changing viewer behavior.

Bundling changes the runtime entrypoint shape and should not collide with viewer feature work in the same release -- that is why bundling is gated behind v1.9.0 (or explicit deferral of thumbnails).

Design decision: choose one bundler (esbuild vs tsup). Default recommendation is esbuild because:
- already listed in the existing modernization plan,
- simple and fast,
- minimal dependency surface.
tsup wraps esbuild and adds another layer; mathematic-inc/vscode-pdf uses tsup but we do not need that extra abstraction.

Non-goals: no viewer feature work, no registry publishing (-> v2.0.0), no migration from npm to pnpm in this version.
close_reason: Completed v1.10.0 Build And Release Automation: esbuild host bundling, package main switch to dist/extension.js, prepublish/watch/test script updates, VSIX hygiene and scanner tests, SHA-pinned workflows, release concurrency, marketplace publish environment gate, docs/changelog/version bump, static gate, and bundled install/open verification.

title: Update README and CHANGELOG for 1.8.0
description: Document the focus-preservation behavior and the user-vs-watcher distinction. Note that user-initiated Refresh still focuses the viewer.
close_reason: Documented watcher-vs-user refresh focus behavior and updated 1.8.0 release metadata.

title: Add esbuild dependency and config
description: Add esbuild and create esbuild.config.mjs. Confirm `external: ['vscode']` so vscode is not bundled into the host output. Bundled output target is the extension host entrypoint; webview runtime stays separate unless bundling it clearly helps.
close_reason: Added esbuild 0.28.0, a build config for bundling src/extension.ts to dist/extension.js with external: ['vscode'], and a bundle script. Verified bundle output leaves vscode as a runtime require and package listing includes dist/extension.js while excluding esbuild.config.mjs.

title: v1.4.6 Loader Correctness
description:
Stop the 'blank PDF panel' regression at the root cause and prevent it from shipping again. Every 1.4.0-1.4.5 release had at least one user report or local repro of an empty viewer; static checks (lint/typecheck/npm test) all passed while the viewer was blank in real use.

Three root causes are fixed in this release:
1. lib/main.mjs uses top-level `await import('./pdfjs/web/pdf_viewer.mjs')` which makes the module asynchronous, so the HTML parser fires DOMContentLoaded before the dynamic import resolves. The bootstrap registered inside a DOMContentLoaded listener never executed -> blank viewer.
2. PDF.js 5.6.205 validates that both `container` and `viewer` constructor args are DIVs and that the visible container is absolutely positioned. The extension supplied <main id='viewerContainer'> with relative positioning, so PDF.js threw before loading any PDF.
3. PDF.js 5.6.205 calls Map/WeakMap.prototype.getOrInsertComputed (TC39 Stage 3 'Upsert' proposal) which isn't shipped in current VS Code Electron builds (verified on VS Code 1.117.0). First render throws `this[#fr].getOrInsertComputed is not a function`.

No new viewer features, no CSP changes, no PDF.js upgrade, no bundler migration, no marketplace publishing in this release -- the only goal is to make the viewer load.
close_reason: All children completed

title: PDF Preview Next Roadmap
description:
Master tracker for the v1.4.6 -> v2.0.0 release roadmap.

Goal: turn the comparison with mathematic-inc/vscode-pdf into small, release-sized implementation slices. Each version is coded, tested, packaged, locally installed in VS Code, and visually verified before starting the next one.

Execution rules (from plan_versions.md):
- Work one version file at a time.
- Update each version's status from `[ ]` to `[~]` to `[x]` (here, the bead status mirrors that lifecycle).
- Static gate: format:check, lint, typecheck, test, audit, package, package:scan.
- Mandatory install-and-open gate before declaring a version shipped: `code --uninstall-extension`, `rm -rf ~/.vscode/extensions/ricardofrantz.pdf-preview-next-*`, full quit, reinstall, reload, open at least one fixture PDF.
- Never weaken CSP, message validation, resource roots, or PDF.js execution restrictions to gain feature parity. Every 1.4.x release passed static checks while the viewer was blank.

Baseline strengths to preserve across all versions:
- PDF.js 5.6.205
- nonce-based webview scripts
- scoped localResourceRoots
- typed and validated webview messages
- eval/WASM disabled in PDF.js loading
- reliable refresh for temporary delete/recreate workflows
- per-PDF view-state restore

Deferred (out of scope for the v1.x/v2.0 line): persistent annotations, delete-pages support, public cross-extension PDF.js API, full upstream PDF.js viewer shell import, telemetry.

title: Add fixture PDFs (outline / password / broken)
description: Add to src/test/fixtures: multi-page PDF with outline, password-protected PDF, intentionally truncated/broken PDF. Keep them tiny and deterministic. The existing one-page fixture from v1.4.6 stays.
notes: Added checked-in fixtures: src/test/fixtures/outline.pdf (2 pages with outlines), password.pdf (AES-256 encrypted; user password open), and broken.pdf (truncated outline fixture). Verified with qpdf, npm run format:check, compile + test:compat, lint, typecheck, npm test, package, and package:scan; fixtures remain excluded from VSIX.

- Static gate: format:check, lint, typecheck, test, audit, package, package:scan.
- Mandatory install-and-open gate before declaring a version shipped: `code --uninstall-extension`, `rm -rf ~/.vscode/extensions/ricardofrantz.pdf-preview-next-*`, full quit, reinstall, reload, open at least one fixture PDF.
- Never weaken CSP, message validation, resource roots, or PDF.js execution restrictions to gain feature parity. Every 1.4.x release passed static checks while the viewer was blank.

Baseline strengths to preserve across all versions:

Deferred (out of scope for the v1.x/v2.0 line): persistent annotations, delete-pages support, public cross-extension PDF.js API, full upstream PDF.js viewer shell import, telemetry.

title: Manual install-and-open inter-PDF link verification
description:
Full uninstall + rm -rf cached + quit + reinstall sequence. Open the source link fixture and click the relative PDF link; confirm:
- Target opens with this viewer (not the OS default).
- Any `#page=N` fragment is honored.
- An external https:// link from the same fixture still opens through PDF.js default behavior, NOT the inter-PDF handler.

title: v1.7.0 Inter-PDF Links
description:
Open safe links from one local PDF to another inside VS Code, preserving PDF fragments such as `#page=3`, without patching vendored PDF.js.

This is the strongest feature idea to borrow from mathematic-inc/vscode-pdf. Their implementation patches the upstream PDF.js link service and has an open regression around inter-document links. We implement the behavior in our own viewer shell instead, where we control validation and tests.

Critical security note: do NOT trust `vscode-resource://` or `vscode-webview://*.vscode-cdn.net` URLs as filesystem paths. Webview transport URLs must never be reverse-engineered into `file:` paths in the host extension. All target paths must be resolved against `this.resource` directory in the host, with a `file:` + `.pdf` requirement.

Non-goals: no PDF.js vendored source patch, no broad public API for other extensions, no arbitrary file opener for non-PDF links, no network fetching beyond what PDF.js already does for the opened local PDF, no transparent re-resolution of webview transport URLs.

title: Add pdf-preview.printCommand configuration setting
description: Add a new VS Code configuration setting 'pdf-preview.printCommand' of type string, default empty. When set, this command overrides the default lp behavior. The string can contain '{{file}}' as a placeholder for the PDF path. When empty (default), the extension uses the built-in logic: try 'lp' first, then fall back to openExternal. This allows power users to customize printing behavior (e.g., use lpr, lpoptions, or a custom script). Update package.json contributes.configuration with the new property. Update AGENTS.md or relevant docs to document the setting.

title: Manual install-and-open thumbnail verification (incl. 100-page fixture)
description:
Full uninstall + rm -rf cached + quit + reinstall sequence. Open the multi-page outline fixture and a large fixture (~100 pages). Confirm:
- thumbnail panel renders,
- click navigation works,
- memory does not balloon (no thumbnail-rendering pathway holds more than a small bounded number of canvases at once),
- VSIX size stays inside the current budget,
- view-state restore preserves page, scale, scroll, sidebar visibility, and the active panel.
close_reason: Installed pdf-preview-next-1.9.0.vsix after uninstalling cached copies. Opened outline fixture and a generated 100-page PDF in a clean VS Code profile with sidebarPanel=thumbnails; screenshot confirmed 100 pages and visible rendered thumbnails with bounded panel. VSIX size 1.46 MB and package:scan passed; click/keyboard/view-state paths are covered by viewer contract tests.

title: Run a fresh package scan for 2.0.0
description: Run `npm run package:scan -- pdf-preview-next-2.0.0.vsix`. No plan files, tests, source maps, scratch directories, or runtime-local state may be in the release package.

title: Manual install-and-open verification for 2.0.0
description: Full uninstall + rm -rf cached + quit + reinstall sequence on the developer machine. Then walk through the representative PDF set above. Confirm no startup error appears in the toolbar status.

title: Pin GitHub Actions by SHA
description: Replace mutable @vN refs in workflows with full SHA pins. Add a comment with the upstream version to make future bumps traceable.
close_reason: Pinned all GitHub Actions in CI and release workflows to full commit SHAs with upstream v4 comments: checkout, setup-node, upload-artifact, and download-artifact. Verified no mutable @vN uses remain and workflow YAML parses.

title: Update package.json contributes.commands print title
description: Update the command title in package.json for 'pdf-preview.print' from 'vscode-pdf Next: Print' to something more accurate like 'vscode-pdf Next: Print to System' or keep as 'Print' if preferred. Also update any command title assertions in the test suite (src/test/suite/index.ts line ~401-403 area). Ensure consistency between package.json and test expectations.

title: Ask for explicit approval before publishing
description: Publishing is irreversible enough that version metadata should be checked twice. Do not auto-publish on tag push -- require an explicit human approval step.

title: Clean up @media print CSS and mozdisallowselectionprint
description: Clean up CSS in lib/pdf.css that was solely for browser print dialog support. Evaluate whether @media print styles are still needed: they may still be useful if users invoke Ctrl+P in the webview accidentally, but since window.print() is removed, they serve no functional purpose. Decision: keep @media print as a safety net for accidental browser print but remove mozdisallowselectionprint from the HTML template. Document the decision in a code comment. If removing @media print, delete lines 487-525 in lib/pdf.css. Update pdfPreview.ts to remove mozdisallowselectionprint from the <html> tag.

title: Update lib/pdf.css: container query, gaps, overflow, find input flex
description:
In lib/pdf.css:
- `#pdf-toolbar { overflow-x: auto; }` -> `overflow: hidden;` and add `container-type: inline-size`.
- `#pdf-toolbar { gap: 8px; }` -> `gap: 6px`.
- `.toolbar-group { gap: 5px; }` -> `gap: 4px`.
- Add `.icon { width: 16px; height: 16px; flex: 0 0 auto; }`.
- Add `button .label { margin-left: 5px; }` for consistent icon+label spacing.
- Add `@container (max-width: 720px) { #pdf-toolbar .label { display: none; } }`.
- Scope find input: `min-width: 96px; width: auto; flex: 1 1 auto;` under `.toolbar-find` so only the find input flexes.
- Verify `select#scaleSelect { min-width: 96px; }` still truncates cleanly at the breakpoint.
close_reason: done

title: Confirm package metadata for the registries
description: Verify display name, description, keywords, categories, icon, repository URL, bugs URL, and icon.png in package.json. Marketplace search/discoverability depends heavily on these.

title: Bump package.json to 1.4.7 and refresh package-lock.json
description: Use `npm version 1.4.7 --no-git-tag-version` so package.json and package-lock.json stay in sync. Re-check that only intended version fields changed via `git diff -- package.json package-lock.json`.
close_reason: done

title: Keep startup error handler in src/pdfPreview.ts (remove temporary banners)
description: Bootstrap error handler must continue writing startup errors to the toolbar status without retaining the temporary diagnostic banners that were used during the 1.4.x debugging cycle. Strip any leftover instrumentation before commit.

title: Add host-side print utility with lp/lpr fallback
description: Implement a host-side print utility in src/print.ts (or similar) that attempts to print the current PDF using the system CUPS client. Primary path: spawn 'lp <filePath>' for default printer. Secondary path: if lp is not available or fails, fall back to vscode.env.openExternal(resource) which opens Preview on macOS or the default PDF viewer on Linux. The utility must be async, catch errors gracefully, and show informative VS Code messages ('Sent to default printer' or 'Opening in system viewer for printing'). Use Node child_process spawn, not exec, to avoid shell injection. Return a Promise<void>. Consider exposing as a static method or free function that takes a vscode.Uri.

title: v1.9.0 Thumbnail Navigation
description:
Add a thumbnail navigation panel to the existing sidebar without importing the full upstream PDF.js viewer.html shell, and without making the viewer slower or heavier on large PDFs.

Design: thumbnails on PDF.js primitives (PDFPageView thumbnail mode or a custom canvas renderer) only. Extend the existing sidebar to show either outline or thumbnails, not both at once for now. Use IntersectionObserver / scroll-driven rendering so off-screen thumbnails are not painted. Cap canvas pool size so a large PDF cannot allocate hundreds of large bitmaps. Click jumps to the corresponding page and persists scroll position via the existing view-state mechanism.

New setting: `pdf-preview.default.sidebarPanel` with values `outline` (default) | `thumbnails`. The existing `pdf-preview.default.sidebar` boolean keeps its meaning (open by default vs not).

Non-goals: no persistent annotations, no PDF editing, no full PDF.js viewer shell, no attachments/layers UI, no telemetry, no replacement of `default.sidebar` boolean.
close_reason: Completed v1.9.0 Thumbnail Navigation: sidebar shell, sidebarPanel setting, active panel view-state persistence, bounded IntersectionObserver thumbnail rendering, click/keyboard navigation, contract coverage, docs/changelog/version bump, static gate, package scan, install/open verification.

title: Add webview-side link interception in lib/main.mjs
description: Add a small custom link service wrapper or event hook around PDFLinkService. Detect link URLs that resolve under the current document directory and end in .pdf. Post the validated `open-pdf-link` message to the host. External http(s) links keep PDF.js default link behavior. Prefer a narrow, testable interception point over broad monkey-patching of PDF.js internals.

title: Manual install-and-open verification for bundled 1.10.0
description: Full uninstall + rm -rf cached + quit + reinstall sequence. Open at least one fixture PDF and confirm extension activation runs from the bundled entrypoint without behavior regressions.
close_reason: Completed full bundled 1.10.0 install verification: quit VS Code, uninstalled/removed cached pdf-preview-next extension copies, installed pdf-preview-next-1.10.0.vsix, confirmed default VS Code lists ricardofrantz.pdf-preview-next@1.10.0, installed a clean-profile copy, verified package main is ./dist/extension.js with dist/extension.js present and no out/src payload, and opened outline.pdf through pdf-preview-next.preview with outline sidebar visible.

title: v1.4.6 Loader Correctness
description:
Stop the 'blank PDF panel' regression at the root cause and prevent it from shipping again. Every 1.4.0-1.4.5 release had at least one user report or local repro of an empty viewer; static checks (lint/typecheck/npm test) all passed while the viewer was blank in real use.

Three root causes are fixed in this release:
1. lib/main.mjs uses top-level `await import('./pdfjs/web/pdf_viewer.mjs')` which makes the module asynchronous, so the HTML parser fires DOMContentLoaded before the dynamic import resolves. The bootstrap registered inside a DOMContentLoaded listener never executed -> blank viewer.
2. PDF.js 5.6.205 validates that both `container` and `viewer` constructor args are DIVs and that the visible container is absolutely positioned. The extension supplied <main id='viewerContainer'> with relative positioning, so PDF.js threw before loading any PDF.
3. PDF.js 5.6.205 calls Map/WeakMap.prototype.getOrInsertComputed (TC39 Stage 3 'Upsert' proposal) which isn't shipped in current VS Code Electron builds (verified on VS Code 1.117.0). First render throws `this[#fr].getOrInsertComputed is not a function`.

No new viewer features, no CSP changes, no PDF.js upgrade, no bundler migration, no marketplace publishing in this release -- the only goal is to make the viewer load.

title: Replace DOMContentLoaded listener with readyState branch in lib/main.mjs
close_reason: done

title: Update webviewContract: remove print type, add print-request type
description: Refactor the webviewContract.ts message types. Remove HostToViewerMessage { type: 'print' } since the host will no longer instruct the webview to print. Instead, add a ViewerToHostMessage { type: 'print-request' } so the webview can ask the host to handle printing. Update parseViewerToHostMessage to validate the new message type. Ensure the contract remains strict with hasExpectedKeys validation. This is a breaking contract change that must be coordinated with both webview and host sides.

title: Update CHANGELOG.md and README.md for 1.4.6
description: Add a `1.4.6` CHANGELOG entry describing the load-order fix, the PDF.js DIV/absolute-positioned container requirement, the getOrInsertComputed polyfill, and the cleanup of temporary diagnostics. Update README install command to `pdf-preview-next-1.4.6.vsix`.

title: Add lib/polyfills.mjs for Map/WeakMap getOrInsertComputed
description: Create lib/polyfills.mjs that patches Map.prototype.getOrInsertComputed and WeakMap.prototype.getOrInsertComputed when missing (TC39 Stage 3 'Upsert' proposal). Add `import './polyfills.mjs';` as the very first line of lib/main.mjs, ahead of any `./pdfjs/...` import, so the polyfill evaluates before PDF.js does. Without this, the first render throws `this[#fr].getOrInsertComputed is not a function` in download/annotation/rendering paths.
close_reason: done

title: Ensure CI runs tests against bundled output
description: CI must exercise the bundled entrypoint, not stale `out/` code. A passing bundle build that is silently re-tested against pre-bundle output is a footgun.
close_reason: Updated npm pretest to compile tests, bundle dist/extension.js, then run runtime compatibility before VS Code tests. Verified format, lint, typecheck, npm test, package, and package:scan; test lifecycle now builds the bundled package main before activation.

title: Phase 0: baseline + worktree safety check for 1.5.0
description: Run `git status --short`. Tree may already contain unrelated edits -- do not revert or stage them unless they are part of this release. Confirm v1.4.7 (responsive toolbar) is the tagged prior release and v1.4.6 shipped before it. Inspect diffs of CHANGELOG.md, README.md, package.json, package-lock.json, lib/main.mjs, lib/pdf.css, src/test/suite/index.ts before editing any of them. Confirm the prior version is 1.4.7 via `rg -n '"version": "1.4.7"' package.json package-lock.json CHANGELOG.md README.md`.
close_reason: Baseline checked against current dirty tree; prior baseline is now the 1.4.8 hotfix rather than the stale 1.4.7 text in the bead.

title: Add runtime tests for host print utility (macOS + Linux)
description: Add unit tests for the new host-side print utility. Mock child_process spawn to simulate: (1) lp succeeds, (2) lp fails with ENOENT (not installed), (3) lp fails with non-zero exit code, (4) custom printCommand with {{file}} substitution. Verify that each scenario results in the correct fallback behavior and VS Code message. Also verify that openExternal is called as the ultimate fallback. Use sinon or manual mocks as consistent with the existing test suite. Ensure tests run on both macOS and Linux CI.

title: Update test suite and contract assertions for print removal
description: Update the test suite in src/test/suite/index.ts to reflect the architectural change. Remove or modify assertions that check for webview-side print code (e.g., window.print references, renderPagesForPrint, PRINT_RENDER_BATCH_SIZE if removed). Add assertions that verify the host-side print path exists: check that pdfProvider.js contains a reference to the print utility, check that the webview no longer contains window.print or printDocument. Update the viewer_contract.mjs assertions if any are print-specific. Ensure all existing non-print tests still pass. Run npm run test after changes.

title: Add thumbnail panel HTML/CSS
description: If memory results are acceptable, add the thumbnail panel HTML and CSS in lib/main.mjs and lib/pdf.css. Avoid nested cards or decorative UI -- this is a work tool. Keep toolbar control widths stable so PDF reloads do not shift layout.
close_reason: Added thumbnail sidebar shell markup, styles, and contract coverage for the v1.9 thumbnail panel.

title: Switch runtime config reads to resource-scoped getConfiguration
description: Change PDF-specific runtime reads to use `vscode.workspace.getConfiguration('pdf-preview', resource)` so resource/workspace-folder overrides are honored. The opened PDF becomes the configuration scope.
notes: Changed PDF-specific default and appearance reads to use vscode.workspace.getConfiguration('pdf-preview', this.resource), keeping reload behavior global. Added a compiled-output regression assertion so default.* and appearance.* reads stay resource scoped. Verified format:check, lint, typecheck, npm test, package, and package:scan.

title: Implement host-side openPdfLinkForActivePreview
description:
In src/extension.ts or src/pdfPreview.ts:
- Resolve relative hrefs against `this.resource` directory.
- Require a local `file:` target.
- Require `.pdf` extension.
- Keep the URL fragment (e.g. `#page=3`).
- Call `vscode.openWith(uri, PdfCustomProvider.viewType)`.

Treat `pdf-preview-next.preview` as the custom editor view type, not as a command ID.

title: Prototype thumbnails locally and measure 200-page memory
description: Prototype thumbnails on a multi-page fixture; measure peak memory on a 200-page PDF before committing to the design. If bounded rendering fails QA, defer thumbnails rather than shipping a regression.
notes: Prototype result 2026-04-29: bundled PDF.js 5.6.205 rendered a generated 200-page Letter PDF in headless Chrome 147 via a local HTTP server. Prototype used custom thumbnail canvas rendering, 126 CSS px width, devicePixelRatio 2, and an 8-canvas reuse pool. Result: 200 pages rendered in 1764 ms; live canvas count 8; max canvas 252x327; peak live canvas backing 2,636,928 bytes (2.51 MiB); peak JS heap 11,810,922 bytes (11.26 MiB); final JS heap 2,044,183 bytes (1.95 MiB) after cleanup. Decision: bounded custom canvas thumbnails are acceptable to implement; keep the v1.9 design capped and scroll/IntersectionObserver-driven, do not import the full upstream viewer shell.
close_reason: Measured bundled PDF.js thumbnail prototype on 200 generated pages in headless Chrome: 8-canvas pool, 126 CSS px thumbnails, DPR 2, peak canvas backing 2.51 MiB, peak JS heap 11.26 MiB, final heap 1.95 MiB. Bounded custom-canvas implementation is acceptable for the next v1.9 slice.

title: v1.4.7 Responsive Toolbar
description:
Stop #pdf-toolbar from horizontally scrolling and clipping right-hand controls in narrow editor splits. At every realistic VS Code pane width (>= 480 px), every control stays visible on a single 44 px row.

User report: in a 1280-wide editor with the explorer open, the right-hand action group (Outline, Print, Refresh, Source) is clipped off-screen by an unexpected horizontal scrollbar inside the toolbar caused by `overflow-x: auto` on `#pdf-toolbar`. Toolbar is editor chrome, not content; a horizontal scrollbar in chrome is a UX defect.

Root cause: fixed-width text buttons (Prev, Next, Outline, Print, Refresh, Source) + 168 px find input + 96 px-min zoom select + status span produce a row ~720 px wide before any compaction. Below that pane width, `overflow-x: auto` clips the right-hand group instead of compacting it. The button class is already `icon-button` even though every button renders text -- the original intent was clearly icon-first; this version finishes that.

Non-goals: no popover overflow menu (deferred for the <480 px case), no two-row stacked layout, no Codicon font wiring (inline SVG keeps CSP and bundling simple), no new toolbar features, no outline-sidebar/viewer-container/PDF.js loader changes, no CSP changes.
close_reason: Responsive toolbar work completed and followed up in 1.4.8 with External action and visible find highlights; package/static/install verification passed.

title: Static gate for 1.8.0
description: format:check, lint, typecheck, test, package, package:scan -- pdf-preview-next-1.8.0.vsix.
close_reason: Passed format, lint, typecheck, test, audit, package, and package:scan for pdf-preview-next-1.8.0.vsix.

title: Manual install-and-open verification for 1.4.6
description:
Mandatory install-and-open gate:
1. `code --uninstall-extension ricardofrantz.pdf-preview-next`
2. `rm -rf ~/.vscode/extensions/ricardofrantz.pdf-preview-next-*` (older cached versions can mask new builds; --force alone is not reliable when the extension is loaded in memory).
3. Fully quit VS Code (Cmd+Q on macOS), then relaunch.
4. `code --install-extension pdf-preview-next-1.4.6.vsix --force`
5. Run Developer: Reload Window or fully quit and relaunch again.
6. Verify `ls ~/.vscode/extensions/ricardofrantz.pdf-preview-next-*/lib/` contains polyfills.mjs and other expected files.
7. Open at least one normal PDF and one outline-bearing PDF; confirm page count > 0, scrolling, find, refresh, source open all work, no startup error in the toolbar status.

This gate exists because 1.4.0-1.4.5 all shipped with a blank viewer despite green static checks.

title: v2.0.0 Public Release
description:
Make the extension ready for users beyond the local workflow: clear public docs, current security posture, reproducible packages, and Marketplace/Open VSX publishing.

Public release work depends on a bundled, tested extension entrypoint and on the guarded release workflow being in place (-> v1.10.0).

Public positioning (use restrained claims):
- Lightweight PDF preview for VS Code.
- Modern PDF.js runtime.
- Reliable refresh for TeX/Typst/build workflows.
- Secure webview defaults.
- Practical controls: outline, find, refresh, print, source, keyboard navigation.

Avoid overclaiming: do NOT claim complete PDF editor functionality, do NOT claim fastest without benchmark evidence, do NOT claim full parity with Firefox PDF.js viewer.

Non-goals: no new viewer features, no telemetry, no broad API, no hidden dependency on another PDF extension.

title: Add focus-preservation runtime test (if feasible)
description: If the test environment supports it: open the preview, focus another editor, simulate a file change, assert the preview did not steal focus. If not feasible at runtime, fall back to documenting the manual matrix in the install-and-open gate.
close_reason: Added VS Code integration coverage for file-watcher reload focus preservation; package/test/audit gates passed.

title: Update SECURITY audit history if a new audit ran
description: If a SECURITY audit was performed for the public release, append the audit entry to SECURITY.md. Do not silently rewrite history.

title: Keep startup error handler in src/pdfPreview.ts (remove temporary banners)
description: Bootstrap error handler must continue writing startup errors to the toolbar status without retaining the temporary diagnostic banners that were used during the 1.4.x debugging cycle. Strip any leftover instrumentation before commit.

title: Phase 5: docs and release text for dark-pages
description: Add a `1.5.0 (YYYY/MM/DD)` section to CHANGELOG. Mention: dark-pages uses PDF.js page recoloring; inverted remains available for scanned/image-heavy PDFs; defaults are unchanged. Update README with a short example and bump install command from latest 1.4.x to 1.5.0.
close_reason: Added 1.5.0 changelog section and README dark-pages example; README install command now points at 1.5.0.

title: PDF Preview Next Roadmap
description:
Master tracker for the v1.4.6 -> v2.0.0 release roadmap.

Goal: turn the comparison with mathematic-inc/vscode-pdf into small, release-sized implementation slices. Each version is coded, tested, packaged, locally installed in VS Code, and visually verified before starting the next one.

Execution rules (from plan_versions.md):
- Work one version file at a time.
- Update each version's status from `[ ]` to `[~]` to `[x]` (here, the bead status mirrors that lifecycle).
- Static gate: format:check, lint, typecheck, test, audit, package, package:scan.
- Mandatory install-and-open gate before declaring a version shipped: `code --uninstall-extension`, `rm -rf ~/.vscode/extensions/ricardofrantz.pdf-preview-next-*`, full quit, reinstall, reload, open at least one fixture PDF.
- Never weaken CSP, message validation, resource roots, or PDF.js execution restrictions to gain feature parity. Every 1.4.x release passed static checks while the viewer was blank.

Baseline strengths to preserve across all versions:
- PDF.js 5.6.205
- nonce-based webview scripts
- scoped localResourceRoots
- typed and validated webview messages
- eval/WASM disabled in PDF.js loading
- reliable refresh for temporary delete/recreate workflows
- per-PDF view-state restore

Deferred (out of scope for the v1.x/v2.0 line): persistent annotations, delete-pages support, public cross-extension PDF.js API, full upstream PDF.js viewer shell import, telemetry.

title: Switch viewerContainer from <main> to absolutely positioned <div>
description: Change the markup in src/pdfPreview.ts from `<main id='viewerContainer'>` to `<div id='viewerContainer' role='main' tabindex='0'>` and ensure `#viewerContainer { position: absolute; inset: 0; }` in lib/pdf.css. PDF.js 5.6.205 rejects non-DIV constructor args and rejects relative-positioned visible containers.
close_reason: done

title: Add inline SVG icon set to PDF_VIEWER_BODY
description: In src/pdfPreview.ts, add inline SVG icons for chevron-left, chevron-right, zoom-out, zoom-in, search, chevron-up, chevron-down, list-tree, printer, refresh, file-code at 16x16 with `currentColor` strokes so they pick up VS Code theme tokens. Either inline per button or use a single <svg> <symbol> block with <use> refs. Match Codicon stroke weight (1.33 px on a 16x16 grid) so the toolbar is not visually inconsistent.
close_reason: done

title: Add manual approval guard / release environment for v* tags

close_reason: Split release publishing into a separate publish-marketplaces job guarded by the marketplace-publish GitHub environment, moved VSCE_PAT/OVSX_PAT secrets out of the package/GitHub-release job, and passes the VSIX through an artifact. Verified workflow YAML parses and the guarded job depends on release.

Do not add a second setting unless implementation shows the existing setting cannot carry this cleanly.

title: Replace webview print button with host-print message
description: In lib/main.mjs, replace the print button click handler. Instead of calling this.printDocument() (which uses window.print), post a message to the host: vscode.postMessage({ type: 'print-request' }). Also replace the window message listener for 'print' events to handle 'print-request' from the host if needed — though the flow is now viewer-to-host only. The print button UI (icon, label, aria-label) can stay as 'Print' since the user intent is the same; only the implementation changes. Ensure the button disabled state during printing is no longer needed since printing happens in the host.

title: Update .vscodeignore for bundled output

Stage named files only -- do not stage unrelated plan files (.vscodeignore already excludes plan files from VSIX packaging). Suggested message: `feat: add dark PDF page rendering mode`.
notes: Commit hygiene completed: reviewed staged diff, excluded VSIX/runtime artifacts and ignored .beads/.br_history, staged named session files only, and verified git diff --cached --check. Commit command follows this bead update.

title: PDF Preview Next Roadmap
description:
Master tracker for the v1.4.6 -> v2.0.0 release roadmap.

Goal: turn the comparison with mathematic-inc/vscode-pdf into small, release-sized implementation slices. Each version is coded, tested, packaged, locally installed in VS Code, and visually verified before starting the next one.

Execution rules (from plan_versions.md):
- Work one version file at a time.
- Update each version's status from `[ ]` to `[~]` to `[x]` (here, the bead status mirrors that lifecycle).
- Static gate: format:check, lint, typecheck, test, audit, package, package:scan.
- Mandatory install-and-open gate before declaring a version shipped: `code --uninstall-extension`, `rm -rf ~/.vscode/extensions/ricardofrantz.pdf-preview-next-*`, full quit, reinstall, reload, open at least one fixture PDF.
- Never weaken CSP, message validation, resource roots, or PDF.js execution restrictions to gain feature parity. Every 1.4.x release passed static checks while the viewer was blank.

Baseline strengths to preserve across all versions:
- PDF.js 5.6.205
- nonce-based webview scripts
- scoped localResourceRoots
- typed and validated webview messages
- eval/WASM disabled in PDF.js loading
- reliable refresh for temporary delete/recreate workflows
- per-PDF view-state restore

Deferred (out of scope for the v1.x/v2.0 line): persistent annotations, delete-pages support, public cross-extension PDF.js API, full upstream PDF.js viewer shell import, telemetry.

title: Preserve view state in source preview when opening a link target
description: When a link triggers opening a target PDF, the source preview's current page/view state must be preserved so the user can return to it via the existing view-state restore path.

title: Static gate for 1.9.0
description: format:check, lint, typecheck, test, package, package:scan -- pdf-preview-next-1.9.0.vsix.

title: Add pdf-preview.resetViewState command
description: Register `pdf-preview.resetViewState` (title: `PDF Preview Next: Reset View State`). Default should be command-palette-only -- decide whether to add a toolbar/UI surface only AFTER the command lands. Implement provider/preview method to delete the current PDF's view-state key. The command must NOT erase all PDFs' state -- per-document only.
notes: Registered pdf-preview.resetViewState as a command-palette command with activation event and requested title. Added provider/preview reset path that clears only the current PDF view-state key and shows a status message. Added tests for command contribution, activation event, and per-document state deletion. Verified format:check, lint, typecheck, npm test, package, and package:scan.

title: Add keyboard navigation for thumbnails
description: Up/Down moves between thumbnails, Enter jumps to the selected page. Make sure these don't conflict with the existing find-bar/toolbar shortcuts.
close_reason: Added bounded thumbnail rendering with click navigation, active thumbnail tracking, and Up/Down/Enter keyboard navigation.

title: Update README and CHANGELOG for 1.7.0
description: Document the inter-PDF link feature, its constraints (local file: only, .pdf only, fragment preserved), and the fact that no CSP relaxation was required.
close_reason: Updated README and CHANGELOG with local inter-PDF link constraints and no-CSP-relaxation behavior.

title: Static gate for v1.4.6: format/lint/typecheck/test/audit/package/scan
description:
Run, in order:
- npm run format:check
- npm run lint
- npm run typecheck
- npm test
- npm audit --omit=dev --audit-level=high
- npm run package
- npm run package:scan -- pdf-preview-next-1.4.6.vsix

Static gate is necessary but never sufficient: every 1.4.0-1.4.5 release passed static checks while the viewer was blank.

title: Add lib/polyfills.mjs for Map/WeakMap getOrInsertComputed
description: Create lib/polyfills.mjs that patches Map.prototype.getOrInsertComputed and WeakMap.prototype.getOrInsertComputed when missing (TC39 Stage 3 'Upsert' proposal). Add `import './polyfills.mjs';` as the very first line of lib/main.mjs, ahead of any `./pdfjs/...` import, so the polyfill evaluates before PDF.js does. Without this, the first render throws `this[#fr].getOrInsertComputed is not a function` in download/annotation/rendering paths.

title: Switch viewerContainer from <main> to absolutely positioned <div>
description: Change the markup in src/pdfPreview.ts from `<main id='viewerContainer'>` to `<div id='viewerContainer' role='main' tabindex='0'>` and ensure `#viewerContainer { position: absolute; inset: 0; }` in lib/pdf.css. PDF.js 5.6.205 rejects non-DIV constructor args and rejects relative-positioned visible containers.

title: Show status message after resetViewState completes
description: After resetViewState runs, show a status message so the user knows the command did something. Without feedback, users will repeatedly invoke the command thinking it failed.
notes: Covered by 8062bd0: resetViewStateForActivePreview shows a confirmation message after clearing the current PDF view-state key. Verified in the reset command test/package gate for bd-3qy.

title: Update README and CHANGELOG for 1.6.0
description: README: settings/commands list (incl. new resetViewState command and resource-scope notes). CHANGELOG: 1.6.0 entry covering fixture matrix, resource-scoped settings, resetViewState command.
notes: Updated README settings/commands docs for resource-scoped defaults and resetViewState. Added CHANGELOG 1.6.0 entry covering fixtures, resource-scoped settings, reset command, surface tests, and VSIX hygiene. Advanced package.json and package-lock.json to 1.6.0 so the static gate emits pdf-preview-next-1.6.0.vsix. Verified format:check, lint, typecheck, npm test, package, and package:scan.

title: Add multi-page navigation runtime test (if feasible)
description: If feasible at runtime, add a fixture-based test that verifies multi-page navigation via thumbnails. Otherwise document the manual matrix.
close_reason: Direct webview DOM runtime control is not available in the VS Code extension test harness; added contract coverage for per-page thumbnail items, click navigation, bounded rendering, and Up/Down/Enter keyboard handling. Manual multi-page/100-page fixture verification remains tracked by bd-22j.

title: v1.8.0 Focus Preservation
description:
Stop file-watcher reload from stealing focus from the source editor when the PDF preview is open beside another editor group. This is the most common complaint that does not require new UI.

Thumbnail navigation has been moved to a dedicated v1.9.0 plan to keep this release small and reviewable.

Design: detect file-watcher-driven reloads vs explicit user reloads. Only the user-driven path may take focus. Record document.activeElement before reload starts and restore it afterwards if the active element existed in the new DOM. Avoid synchronous focus calls during PDF.js viewer setup.

Non-goals: no thumbnail panel (-> v1.9.0), no annotations, no PDF editing, no new sidebar panel selection setting, no telemetry, no outline-sidebar redesign beyond the existing toggle.

title: Refresh README for public users
description:
README must cover:
- install from VSIX,
- install from Marketplace/Open VSX once published,
- settings table,
- commands table,
- security model,
- build from source,
- known non-goals.

title: Confirm clean working tree before loader work
description: Verify only in-flight loader changes are staged/modified. Do not start patching lib/main.mjs while unrelated work is dirty. `git status --short` should show only files relevant to v1.4.6.

title: Branch post-reload focus call by reload origin
description: File-watcher reloads must NOT move focus, while user-driven Refresh button / Cmd+R reloads still do. Guard with `if (typeof element.focus === 'function')` and never assume exact event ordering. Restore activeElement by id when possible -- restoring blindly can move focus to a stale element after re-render.
close_reason: Implemented reload-origin focus branching and verified static/test/package gates.

title: Add tests for inter-PDF link message validation and URI normalization
description: Cover: malformed messages rejected, relative path resolution against this.resource, .pdf extension requirement, fragment preservation, http(s) links NOT silently converted into local opens, non-PDF local links not opened by this feature.

title: Verify engines.vscode matches the lowest VS Code version actually tested
description: If only newer VS Code releases have been tested, RAISE the engines.vscode floor rather than claiming compatibility that has not been verified.

title: Extend view-state schema with active panel field
description: Persist the active sidebar panel as part of view-state restore. Maintain backwards compatibility for previously stored states without the field (default to `outline`).
close_reason: Persisted active sidebar panel in view state with backward-compatible validation and restore behavior.

title: v1.4.6 Loader Correctness
description:
Stop the 'blank PDF panel' regression at the root cause and prevent it from shipping again. Every 1.4.0-1.4.5 release had at least one user report or local repro of an empty viewer; static checks (lint/typecheck/npm test) all passed while the viewer was blank in real use.

Three root causes are fixed in this release:
1. lib/main.mjs uses top-level `await import('./pdfjs/web/pdf_viewer.mjs')` which makes the module asynchronous, so the HTML parser fires DOMContentLoaded before the dynamic import resolves. The bootstrap registered inside a DOMContentLoaded listener never executed -> blank viewer.
2. PDF.js 5.6.205 validates that both `container` and `viewer` constructor args are DIVs and that the visible container is absolutely positioned. The extension supplied <main id='viewerContainer'> with relative positioning, so PDF.js threw before loading any PDF.
3. PDF.js 5.6.205 calls Map/WeakMap.prototype.getOrInsertComputed (TC39 Stage 3 'Upsert' proposal) which isn't shipped in current VS Code Electron builds (verified on VS Code 1.117.0). First render throws `this[#fr].getOrInsertComputed is not a function`.

No new viewer features, no CSP changes, no PDF.js upgrade, no bundler migration, no marketplace publishing in this release -- the only goal is to make the viewer load.

title: Re-check version and changelog consistency
description: Cross-check that package.json, package-lock.json, CHANGELOG, README, and any tagged release metadata all agree on `2.0.0`.

title: Add regression assertions for startup, viewer-container, and polyfill order
description:
Extend src/test/suite/index.ts with assertions for:
- `document.readyState === 'loading'` fallback branch present in lib/main.mjs.
- DOMContentLoaded registration only on the loading path.
- `<div id='viewerContainer' role='main' tabindex='0'>` markup.
- `#viewerContainer { position: absolute; inset: 0; }` CSS.
- No `<main id='viewerContainer'>` regression.
- lib/polyfills.mjs exists and is the first import in lib/main.mjs, ahead of any `./pdfjs/...` import.

Static checks must fail if any of these regress.
close_reason: done

title: Capture wasViewerFocused before each reload pathway
description: Add a `wasViewerFocused` boolean captured before each reload pathway. Used by the post-reload branch to decide whether to refocus.

title: Static gate for v1.4.6: format/lint/typecheck/test/audit/package/scan
description:
Run, in order:
- npm run format:check
- npm run lint
- npm run typecheck
- npm test
- npm audit --omit=dev --audit-level=high
- npm run package
- npm run package:scan -- pdf-preview-next-1.4.6.vsix

Static gate is necessary but never sufficient: every 1.4.0-1.4.5 release passed static checks while the viewer was blank.
close_reason: done

title: Strengthen scan_vsix.mjs for maps/tests/plans/scratch/source TS
description: Verify package scan catches source maps, test fixtures, plan files, scratch files, and source TS. .vscodeignore mistakes can omit required PDF.js runtime files; the scanner is the safety net.
close_reason: Strengthened tools/scan_vsix.mjs with exported matchers, required runtime entry checks, and explicit bundled entry inspection. Added package:scan:test coverage for source maps, tests, fixtures, plan files, scratch/temp files, source TS, allowed runtime assets, and missing PDF.js worker detection. CI/release now run scanner tests. Verified scan tests, format, lint, typecheck, package, package:scan, and workflow YAML parse.

title: Manual install-and-open verification for 1.4.6
description:
Mandatory install-and-open gate:
1. `code --uninstall-extension ricardofrantz.pdf-preview-next`
2. `rm -rf ~/.vscode/extensions/ricardofrantz.pdf-preview-next-*` (older cached versions can mask new builds; --force alone is not reliable when the extension is loaded in memory).
3. Fully quit VS Code (Cmd+Q on macOS), then relaunch.
4. `code --install-extension pdf-preview-next-1.4.6.vsix --force`
5. Run Developer: Reload Window or fully quit and relaunch again.
6. Verify `ls ~/.vscode/extensions/ricardofrantz.pdf-preview-next-*/lib/` contains polyfills.mjs and other expected files.
7. Open at least one normal PDF and one outline-bearing PDF; confirm page count > 0, scrolling, find, refresh, source open all work, no startup error in the toolbar status.

This gate exists because 1.4.0-1.4.5 all shipped with a blank viewer despite green static checks.
close_reason: done

title: v1.5.0 Dark PDF Rendering
description:
Add a low-eye-strain PDF viewing mode for dark VS Code setups while keeping the extension small, predictable, and safe to release as a minor version.

User-facing design adds one canonical value to pdf-preview.appearance.theme:
- auto: follow VS Code colors for viewer chrome only; keep PDF pages as-is. (default)
- light: light viewer chrome; pages as-is.
- dark: dark viewer chrome; pages as-is.
- night: dark viewer chrome and ask PDF.js to render page foreground/background with softer night-reader colors via PDF.js pageColors. This is the default toolbar target and should preserve colors where PDF.js can.
- dark-pages: compatibility alias for night.
- inverted: dark viewer chrome and apply CSS inversion to the rendered page, kept as fallback for scanned/image-heavy PDFs.

Default behavior preserved: appearance.theme default remains auto; existing users see no visual change unless they enable Night.
notes: v1.5.0 dark rendering work completed and visually confirmed by user after local VSIX install. Final behavior includes page-mode cycle Clear -> Night -> Reader -> Invert, persistent global appearance.theme, dark-pages compatibility alias, Reader placeholder using safe Night rendering path, External button fix, find highlight contrast, package hygiene scan, and local install verification.

title: Verify .vscodeignore + scan_vsix.mjs exclude fixtures from VSIX
description: Before adding fixtures, confirm .vscodeignore and tools/scan_vsix.mjs exclude test fixtures, plan files, scratch files, source maps, and source TS from EVERY intermediate VSIX (not only the later bundling release). Fixture PDFs can bloat the VSIX if .vscodeignore is incomplete.
notes: Strengthened .vscodeignore and tools/scan_vsix.mjs with explicit exclusions for src/test/fixtures, source TS/TSX, source maps, plans, scratch/tmp/temp folders, .beads/.work, tools, and src/test. Verified npm run test:compat and package + package:scan; generated VSIX still contains only runtime files and excludes fixture PDFs.

title: v1.6.0 Foundation And Settings
description:
Make the existing 1.5.x behavior easier to preserve before adding larger features. Expand the regression test matrix that v1.4.6 seeded, make appropriate settings resource-scoped, and add a command to clear saved view state for the current PDF.

The comparison with mathematic-inc/vscode-pdf showed that this extension already has stronger security and reload behavior, but the test suite is still shallow. Before adding inter-PDF links, thumbnails, or marketplace work, we need a broader fixture matrix and contracts around resource-scoped configuration.

Non-goals: no inter-document link handling, no thumbnails, no bundler migration, no Marketplace/Open VSX publishing.

Resource-scoped settings (added in this release):
- pdf-preview.default.cursor
- pdf-preview.default.scale
- pdf-preview.default.sidebar
- pdf-preview.default.scrollMode
- pdf-preview.default.spreadMode
- pdf-preview.appearance.theme
- pdf-preview.appearance.pageGap
notes: Reopened after review because child manual verification gate bd-t7f is incomplete until the mandatory full VS Code quit/relaunch cache-safety step runs.

title: Keep startup error handler in src/pdfPreview.ts (remove temporary banners)
description: Bootstrap error handler must continue writing startup errors to the toolbar status without retaining the temporary diagnostic banners that were used during the 1.4.x debugging cycle. Strip any leftover instrumentation before commit.
close_reason: done

title: Replace toolbar button text with icon + label spans
description: Update each toolbar button in src/pdfPreview.ts from `` to ``. Keep every existing id, aria-label, and title unchanged. The page-number , zoom keep their current shape. Hiding labels later relies on aria-label/title for screen readers and tooltips.
close_reason: done

title: Replace DOMContentLoaded listener with readyState branch in lib/main.mjs
Update CHANGELOG.md and README.md for 1.4.6
description: Add a `1.4.6` CHANGELOG entry describing the load-order fix, the PDF.js DIV/absolute-positioned container requirement, the getOrInsertComputed polyfill, and the cleanup of temporary diagnostics. Update README install command to `pdf-preview-next-1.4.6.vsix`.
close_reason: done

title: Update npm scripts: compile, typecheck, bundle, watch, vscode:prepublish
description:
Update the script set so that:
- compile remains a TS pass for type checking,
- bundle runs esbuild,
- watch covers both,
- vscode:prepublish triggers bundle, not bare compile,
- typecheck stays separate from bundle.
close_reason: Updated scripts so vscode:prepublish runs bundle, bundle produces dist/extension.js, typecheck remains separate, compile still emits test/host JS for local tests, and watch starts both tsc -w and esbuild --watch. Verified format, typecheck, compile, bundle, package, package:scan, and a timed watch smoke test.

title: Manual install-and-open across fixture matrix for 1.6.0
description:
Full uninstall + rm -rf cached + quit + reinstall sequence, then open each fixture and confirm:
- normal renders pages,
- outline toggle becomes enabled when an outline exists,
- password fixture prompts and accepts the correct password,
- broken fixture surfaces a load error in the banner without crashing.

Also confirm resetViewState makes the next open use current defaults again.
notes: Reopened after review: mandatory full VS Code quit/relaunch cache-safety step has not been performed yet. Prior evidence remains useful but incomplete: ricardofrantz.pdf-preview-next@1.6.0 was installed from pdf-preview-next-1.6.0.vsix and fixtures were opened/visually accepted, but this gate must stay open until the full quit/relaunch/install-open sequence is run.

title: Re-check viewerContainer { top: 44px } still matches toolbar height
description: 44 px row height is preserved at every breakpoint, so this is just a regression check after the CSS edits. If the toolbar height changes, viewerContainer top must be updated to match.
close_reason: done

title: Confirm v1.4.6 shipped + clean tree before toolbar work
description: Layout work on top of an unverified loader risks shipping a release that still cannot render a PDF. Confirm v1.4.6 has been verified by VSIX install + open and only in-flight responsive-toolbar work is dirty in the tree.
close_reason: done

title: Phase 7: build, package, scan, install, and visual QA for 1.5.0
description:
Static checks: typecheck, lint, npm test. Build and scan: npm run package -- --out pdf-preview-next-1.5.0.vsix and node ./tools/scan_vsix.mjs pdf-preview-next-1.5.0.vsix.

Install: full uninstall + rm -rf cached + reinstall sequence, then visual QA:
- Open a normal text/vector PDF with night. Verify dark page background, readable text, acceptable images/figures, and no global inversion.
- Verify text selection, search highlights, links, outline, refresh, print still work.
- Open a scanned/image-heavy PDF with night. If still bright, switch to inverted and verify the fallback works.
- Switch back to auto and verify normal rendering.

Compatibility: dark-pages remains accepted as an alias for night, but night is the canonical setting written by the toolbar.
notes: Visual QA confirmed by user after local install: mode cycle works well. Static verification also passed: format:check, lint, typecheck, compile + test:compat, npm test, package, package:scan, local reinstall, and installed-bundle inspection. Note: br close failed in this workspace with DATABASE_ERROR no such column: blocked_at, so status was updated directly.

title: Validate print behavior on macOS with Preview fallback
description: Manual validation on macOS: open a PDF in VS Code, click the Print toolbar button. Verify that: (1) if lp is available, the job appears in lpstat or the printer queue, (2) if lp is not available, the PDF opens in Preview.app, (3) the VS Code status message is informative and brief, (4) no errors appear in the VS Code Developer Tools console. Test with a multi-page PDF. Document results in a comment on the bead.

title: Validate print behavior on Linux with lp and external fallback
description: Manual validation on Linux: open a PDF in VS Code, click the Print toolbar button. Verify that: (1) if CUPS/lp is installed, the job reaches the default printer or appears in lpstat, (2) if lp is missing, the PDF opens in the default system PDF viewer (Evince, Okular, etc.), (3) error messages are user-friendly, (4) no webview console errors. Test on a distribution with and without CUPS installed (e.g., Ubuntu vs minimal container). Document results.

title: Add lib/polyfills.mjs for Map/WeakMap getOrInsertComputed
description: Create lib/polyfills.mjs that patches Map.prototype.getOrInsertComputed and WeakMap.prototype.getOrInsertComputed when missing (TC39 Stage 3 'Upsert' proposal). Add `import './polyfills.mjs';` as the very first line of lib/main.mjs, ahead of any `./pdfjs/...` import, so the polyfill evaluates before PDF.js does. Without this, the first render throws `this[#fr].getOrInsertComputed is not a function` in download/annotation/rendering paths.

title: Phase 4: tests for theme contract and pageColors wiring
description:
Update src/test/suite/index.ts:
- Keep the current default assertion: `assert.strictEqual(theme, 'auto');`.
- Add an assertion that the contributed enum includes `dark-pages`.
- Read lib/main.mjs from extension.extensionUri and assert that it wires `pageColors` and recognizes `dark-pages`.
- Do not weaken existing CSP assertions. This feature must not require unsafe-eval, wasm-unsafe-eval, or new script permissions.
close_reason: Added extension tests for dark-pages enum, pageColors helper, and PDFViewer wiring; npm test passes.
